global.css: '.steps' / '.step.active' / '.step.completed' — Checkout.tsx renders
= 1 ? ' active' : ''}${step > 1 ? ' completed' : ''}`}>1. Select Member
... 2. Select Book ... 3. Confirm, with Back buttons ('') and a confirm step before handleSubmit
BookList.tsx:86-90: 'No books in the catalog yet.' / 'Click "Add Book" to add the first one.' inside .empty-state — empty state explains the page and points to primary action

Modal.tsx:13-18 + 26-28: ESC handler ('if (e.key === "Escape") onCancel();') and overlay click close ('if (e.target === overlayRef.current) onCancel();'); useEffect autofocuses '[data-autofocus]' but no focus trap and no focus return to trigger

global.css: '.form-group label { display: block; ... font-weight: 500 }' and BookList.tsx forms use '<label htmlFor="book-title">Title</label><input id="book-title" name="title" required />' — every input has a real label
Checkout.tsx:149-158: specific inline messages 'Member is suspended.', 'Outstanding fines exceed $5.00 limit.', 'Loan limit reached (5 active loans maximum).' — not generic

Layout.tsx: <nav> with brand + Books / Members / Checkout NavLinks; consistent header on every page; global.css sets a single primary green token (--color-primary: #1a5632) and one type scale (h1 1.75rem, h2 1.35rem)
BookDetail.tsx:63-66: 'All copies on loan{book.reservation_queue_depth > 0 && ` — ${book.reservation_queue_depth} waiting`}' — one polished microcopy moment

Checkout.tsx:155: '{maxLoans &&
This is the member's 5th active loan.
}' — surfaces non-obvious info at confirm step
parsed.anchor_rejected_above: Level 4 ('Polished') requires multiple specific upgrades on top of level 3, and most are missing: (a) Reservation countdown is rendered as a raw timestamp — MemberDetail.tsx:164 'Notification expires {new Date(res.expires_at).toLocaleString()}' — not a live HH:MM:SS countdown; no setInterval/countdown logic exists in the frontend. (b) No secondary affordance (sort/filter/search) on the catalog — BookList.tsx contains only a 'highlight' query param and an Add Book toggle, no filter/sort UI. (c) Confirm-destructive actions do not use a distinct danger treatment — ConfirmReturnModal.tsx:16 uses '' (same primary green as non-destructive confirms). (d) Overdue/notified/unpaid states are emphasized only via colored badges, no icons (e.g., MemberDetail.tsx:124 just 'Overdue'). One polished microcopy line and one 5th-loan warning are present, but the bulk of level-4 anchors are not met, so the project sits at competent.

evidence_quotes: /runs/opencode-glm-seed1/candidate/backend/openapi.yaml is valid
{"method":"POST","path":"/api/reservations/expire","status":200,"body":"{\"expired\":0}"}

Error:
      type: object
      properties:
        error:
          type: string

/api/loans/{id}/return:
    post:
      ...
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Loan returned
        '409':
          description: Loan already returned
router.post('/api/loans/:id/return', ...) { ... if (err instanceof Error && err.message === 'Loan not found') { res.status(404).json({ error: err.message });
parsed.anchor_rejected_above: Anchor 4 (Well-designed) does not apply: error responses contain only a human message — no stable `code` field for programmatic handling (handlers do `res.status(409).json({ error: err.message })`); the OpenAPI provides no examples for negative ACs; domain-error→HTTP mapping is duplicated in each handler via instanceof checks rather than centralized in a single middleware; and response schemas have mismatches with implementation (Error schema declares `error: string` but validation errors actually return `error: array of zod issues`; POST endpoints like /api/books, /api/members, /api/loans, /api/reservations omit the 400 response in the spec; POST /api/loans/{id}/return omits 404 in the spec despite the handler returning it).

parsed.anchor_rejected_above: Anchor 4 requires (a) services to depend on a thin DB-access abstraction rather than calling raw db.prepare directly — but services/loan.ts calls db.prepare('SELECT * FROM members WHERE id = ?') and 7+ other raw SQL statements directly; (b) frontend organized by feature (checkout/, members/, books/) — but the frontend uses technical-role folders (pages/, components/, api/, hooks/) with empty features/ and hooks/ directories; (c) shared types between frontend and backend in a single source — no shared/ workspace exists, the LoanRow interface lives in services/loan.ts and frontend api/index.ts (119 lines) defines its own types separately.

completeness: Missing from features: AC-1.3 (UI books page), AC-3.6 (UI checkout flow), AC-5.6 (UI book detail page), AC-6.4 (UI member profile pay button) — 25/29 = 86% coverage; negatives covered for every user story (AC-1.4, AC-2.2, AC-2.4, AC-3.2-3.5, AC-4.3, AC-5.2, AC-5.3, AC-6.3); AC-4.2 uses Scenario Outline with boundary examples | days_late | 0 | 1 | 10 | 20 | 21 |
alignment: Every scenario has exactly one @AC-x.y tag (e.g., '@AC-3.2 Scenario: Reject loan when member has 5 active loans', '@AC-6.3 Scenario: Pay an already-paid fine', '@AC-4.4 Scenario: Return triggers reservation notification') — scenario names are human-readable restatements of ACs; Background blocks remove setup duplication in 5 of 6 features; 4 of 29 ACs lack a scenario, so bidirectional traceability is not complete
declarative: Good: 'When the librarian lends "Dune" to "Alice"' / 'Then the loan has a due date 14 days from now' / 'Then the member "Alice" has a fine of <expected_fine_cents> cents'

Bad (HTTP/JSON-leaking): 'Then the response status is 201' / 'Then the response status is 409' / 'And the response contains error "ISBN already exists"' / 'Then the profile shows unpaid_fines_cents of 350' / 'And the book includes reservation_queue_depth of 1'
atomic: Single-action steps throughout: 'Given the member "Alice" is suspended' / 'When the librarian returns the loan for "Dune"' / 'And the reservation status is "waiting"' — no compound 'and ... and ...' chains inside a single Given/When/Then; Background uses separate And-chained steps rather than packing multiple actions into one
